summary: Graph-to-Code (GTC) is a React/TypeScript SPA (Vite) for building architecture diagrams on a canvas, then generating code/docs from them via Claude. State has two parallel systems: Zustand (source of truth) + React Flow internal state — both must stay in sync. Backends: users-auth (port 8080), canvas-service (port 8082), app-service, build-agent (all Go), proxied by Vite.
key_conventions: All diagram state lives in src/hooks/useDiagram.ts (Zustand). Never hold node/edge truth outside it.
Canvas RF state (useNodesState/useEdgesState) mirrors Zustand but is initialized once from initialNodes/initialEdges props at mount — it does NOT re-init if props change.
canvas-api uses from/to for edges; the store uses sourceNodeId/targetNodeId — transformation happens in useCanvasSync.
Extended node data (contracts, endpoints, embedded-canvas) stored in config under underscore-prefixed keys (_contracts, _endpoints, _hasEmbedded).
All edges use type: 'smoothstep'. Custom node type 'arch' maps to NodeCard.
Node handles have explicit IDs: id='input' (target, left) and id='output' (source, right).
ZoneLayer and DrawZoneOverlay must be children of <ReactFlow> to use useViewport()/useReactFlow().
Drag-drop uses window.__canvasAddNode(type, clientX, clientY) because DndContext wraps the page.
Auth token stored in localStorage key 'auth_token'. Canvas API reads it via authHeaders().
